question: How many of our customers are repeat buyers versus one-time buyers?
sql:
SELECT
  IF(order_count > 1, 'Repeat buyer', 'One-time buyer') AS customer_type,
  COUNT(*) AS customers,
  SUM(order_count) AS total_orders
FROM (
  SELECT user_id, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_count
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.thelook_ecommerce.orders`
  GROUP BY user_id
)
GROUP BY customer_type
ORDER BY customers DESC
report: Of about 80,100 customers, 49,966 (62%) bought only once while 30,136 (38%) are repeat buyers. Yet repeat buyers account for 75,528 of the ~125,500 total orders (60%) - so the 38% who return generate the majority of order volume. The takeaway: converting one-time buyers into repeat buyers is the single biggest growth lever.
